title: Tube-free Agar Overlays: Rapid Plaque Assays with Fewer Steps and Materials
author: Kathryn Kauffman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.j8ncrve
materials: Equilibrate the top agar to 50-52°C: Place the beaker-waterbath containing the bottle of molten top agar and stirbar onto a hot plate and activate gentle stirring, place a thermometer into the beaker-waterbath and leave it there to wait for equilibration to 50-52°C (Figure 1). a Figure 1. Top agar bottle equilibrated in beaker-waterbath. Note: This will require setting the heat block to a temperature greater than the target temperature, for example up to 85°C, but this is dependent on specific heat block models and must be determined by the user.

steps.title: Prepare a beaker-waterbath
steps.instruction: Prepare a beaker-waterbath: Place bottle of top agar into a glass beaker and add water to the beaker up to the level of the top agar in the bottle; for example, a 500 ml glass bottle into a 1l beaker.

steps.title: Ensure that the lid of the...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the lid of the top agar bottle is slightly loose to allow for venting during microwaving. (

steps.title: Remove top agar bottle from the microwave with heat-safe gloves
steps.instruction: Remove top agar bottle from the microwave with heat-safe gloves. (

steps.title: Do not swirl the top agar...
steps.instruction: Do not swirl the top agar bottle while removing from microwave as this may cause sudden boil-over resulting in burns.

steps.title: Add virus
steps.instruction: Add virus: Pipette 10-100 ul of virus-containing solution directly into the host droplet on the bottom agar. a

steps.title: Add molten top agar
steps.instruction: Add molten top agar: Remove the lid of the top agar bottle briefly to pipette out 2 ml of molten top agar using either a 5 ml pipette or serological pipette, and sterile technique. Pipette the 2 ml of molten top agar from the bottle directly onto the bottom agar next to the droplet of hosts and viruses. a

steps.title: Make overlay
steps.instruction: Make overlay: Swirl the plate vigorously but briefly to mix the bacteria and virus and molten top agar and to spread it across the plate

steps.title: Add host
steps.instruction: Add host: Pipette 100 ul of overnight host culture directly onto the bottom agar . a

steps.title: insufficiently melted top agar, which gives...
steps.instruction: insufficiently melted top agar, which gives rise to matte lawns instead of glossy smooth lawns, and

steps.title: taking too long to swirl the...
steps.instruction: taking too long to swirl the agar thus allowing it to cool down and set before the mixing is completed. Note: As in all plating assays, it is recommended that control plates be included to ensure that:

steps.title: the agar overlay is contaminant free...
steps.instruction: the agar overlay is contaminant free at the start and finish of the experiment (include only top agar in these controls),

steps.title: the bacterial stock is virus-free (include...
steps.instruction: the bacterial stock is virus-free (include only bacteria and top agar in thee controls), and

steps.title: the virus stock is not contaminated...
steps.instruction: the virus stock is not contaminated with cells (include only virus stock and top agar in these controls).
